ocp_deployment_guide.overview: This guide defines when and how the DISH SDLC mode may use the OpenShift MCP server for automated deployment.

mcp_server_config.note: Authentication to the OpenShift MCP server is handled by the API key configured in the local `.bob/mcp.json` file. Bob must never load this from `.env`.

required_acceptance_criteria.criterion: Automated deployment required after SonarQube quality gate passes

deployment_trigger.rule: Do not infer deployment intent from vague language.

guardrails.rule: Deploy only after local tests/build validation succeeds.
Deploy only after the SonarQube quality gate passes.
Deploy only to a namespace returned by the OpenShift MCP server allowlist.
Use only safe, namespaced manifests and approved BuildConfig-based flows.
Prefer Git-source BuildConfig flows. Do NOT use binary build upload of any kind — it is unreliable for large Maven projects due to file upload size and timeout constraints.

Never use Java preview features anywhere — not in Java source code, not in pom.xml compilerArgs, and not in the Dockerfile CMD/ENTRYPOINT. The application must start with a plain `java -jar` without --enable-preview. A standard OCP runtime image does not enable preview features and the app will fail to start if any preview feature is used.
Do not assume `curl` or other debugging tools exist in the runtime image for health checks unless they are explicitly installed.
Keep Dockerfile runtime port, Service targetPort, Route targetPort, and application server port aligned.

Before triggering a build, push all modernized source code and the generated Dockerfile to the participant's Gitea repository using git push. The build will fetch source from Gitea — not from the local filesystem.
Never use the `oc` CLI for any step. Use only MCP server tools (ocp.*) and git CLI commands.
Use ocp.start_build to trigger the Git-source build via the OpenShift API. Do not use oc start-build.
If OCP MCP RBAC or namespace authorization fails, stop and report the failure instead of falling back to local cluster commands.
If deployment verification fails, update Jira with the failure and stop before marking the story done.

A Deployment manifest must define exactly ONE container in spec.template.spec.containers — the application container. Never generate or apply a Deployment that has more than one container unless a sidecar is explicitly required. Reason: applying a new Deployment manifest on top of an existing one that had a differently-named container (e.g. old name was "ecommerce-app", new name is "app") results in both containers being present in the pod. Both try to bind the same port and the app fails with "Port 8080 already in use". Before applying a Deployment manifest, check the existing container names with ocp.get_resource and ensure the new manifest uses the same container name OR replaces the containers array entirely with a single entry.

STALE DEPLOYMENT CLEANUP: Before applying any new Deployment, use ocp.delete_resource to remove any existing Deployment in the namespace that has a different name than the one being deployed. Wait for the old pods to terminate before applying the new manifest. This prevents two pods from competing for port 8080 on the same node, which causes CrashLoopBackOff in the new pod.

BUILDCONFIG GITEA SECRET: Every BuildConfig manifest MUST include spec.source.sourceSecret.name pointing to the pre-existing gitea secret in the namespace. Never omit this field. Never attempt to create a Secret resource — RBAC does not permit it. Discover the secret name by inspecting existing Secrets in the namespace before writing the BuildConfig manifest.

APPLICATION URLS IN SUMMARY: After ocp.get_route succeeds, ALWAYS include the following URLs in both the Bob chat summary AND the Jira completion comment, regardless of any earlier warnings: - Main: https://<route-host> - Swagger UI: https://<route-host>/swagger-ui/index.html - Health: https://<route-host>/actuator/health If ocp.get_route fails, explicitly state "Route not yet available — check OCP console" in the summary. Never silently omit URLs.

recommended_tool_sequence.step: ocp.list_allowed_namespaces — verify the target namespace is in the allowlist before proceeding
Generate or update the application Dockerfile in `lab1/modernized-ecommerce-app/Dockerfile`
Generate or update `lab1/modernized-ecommerce-app/settings.xml` with Nexus mirror config (see maven_nexus_mirror section). This file MUST exist before the git commit step.

CLEANUP — Before generating or applying any Deployment manifest, list all existing Deployments in the namespace using ocp.get_resource. If ANY Deployment exists whose name differs from the one you are about to apply (e.g. a generic "app" Deployment left over from a previous run), delete it first using ocp.delete_resource. Only ONE Deployment should exist in the namespace when you apply the new one. Reason: Kubernetes merges Deployment patches by container name. If two Deployments in the same namespace both schedule pods that bind port 8080, the node sees a port conflict and the newer pod enters CrashLoopBackOff. Deleting the stale Deployment removes its pods cleanly before the new rollout begins.

GITEA SECRET — Before writing the BuildConfig manifest, discover the pre-existing Gitea source secret in the namespace by calling ocp.get_resource for Secrets and finding the one whose name matches the pattern `gitea*` or `lab-gitea*`. Use that secret name as the value of spec.source.sourceSecret.name in the BuildConfig. NEVER attempt to create a Secret. NEVER omit sourceSecret from the BuildConfig. If no gitea secret is found in the namespace, stop and report: "No Gitea source secret found in namespace <namespace>. Ask the lab instructor to create it before retrying." Do not proceed with the build.
Generate or update OpenShift manifests (Deployment, Service, Route, ImageStream, BuildConfig). The BuildConfig MUST include spec.source.sourceSecret.name set to the secret discovered above.

Read `.bob/gitea.json` to obtain the Gitea baseUrl, username, and token. Construct the authenticated remote URL as: https://<username>:<token>@<baseUrl-host>/<org>/<repo>.git. Use this URL only for the push — do not persist it to `.git/config`.
Run pre_commit_checklist verifications, then git add + git commit + git push <authenticated-url>

ocp.apply_manifest_bundle — apply the ImageStream, updated BuildConfig (with contextDir: lab1/modernized-ecommerce-app, dockerfilePath: Dockerfile, and sourceSecret), Deployment, Service, and Route into the target namespace
ocp.start_build — trigger the Git-source build via the OpenShift API (namespace + buildConfigName). OCP will clone from Gitea internally.
ocp.wait_for_build — pass the build name returned by ocp.start_build to monitor completion
ocp.restart_deployment when an image change does not automatically trigger rollout
ocp.get_route — ALWAYS call this step even if earlier steps had warnings. Capture the full route hostname.
ocp.get_recent_events or ocp.get_pod_logs if verification fails

gitea_credentials.description: Bob reads `.bob/gitea.json` to obtain credentials for pushing to the participant's Gitea repository. This file must be present and populated before deployment runs.

fields.field: Participant's Gitea personal access token or password

gitea_credentials.usage: Construct the authenticated push URL as: https://<username>:<token>@<gitea-host>/<org>/<repo>.git Pass this URL directly to `git push` — do not store it in `.git/config` to avoid persisting credentials on disk. Example: git push https://hbavaria:mytoken@gitea.apps.example.com/lab/lab-app-hbavaria.git
gitea_credentials.error_handling: If `.bob/gitea.json` is missing or contains placeholder values, stop and ask the participant to populate `.bob/gitea.json` with their Gitea credentials before retrying. Do not attempt the push without valid credentials.

maven_nexus_mirror.description: Maven builds run inside the OCP build pod with no persistent local repository. Without a Nexus mirror, every build re-downloads all dependencies from Maven Central, which is slow and fails if the cluster has no direct internet access. Always configure Maven to use the in-cluster Nexus proxy so dependencies are cached in Nexus and served from within the cluster on subsequent builds.

required_steps.step: Create `lab1/modernized-ecommerce-app/settings.xml` with Nexus configured as the Maven mirror:
In the Dockerfile, COPY settings.xml before running mvn and pass it with -s:
required_steps.dockerfile_snippet: COPY settings.xml /root/.m2/settings.xml RUN mvn -s /root/.m2/settings.xml clean package -DskipTests

rules.rule: Always generate settings.xml alongside the Dockerfile. Never omit it.
Always use `mvn -s /root/.m2/settings.xml` in the Dockerfile RUN command. Never run plain `mvn` without -s in the OCP build context.
The settings.xml must use `mirrorOf: *` so ALL repositories (including Spring, JBoss, etc.) are proxied through Nexus.

Commit settings.xml to Gitea along with the Dockerfile so OCP can access it during the git-source build. Always verify settings.xml is included in `git status --short` before running `git push` — a missing settings.xml in Gitea will cause the OCP build to fail even if the COPY instruction in the Dockerfile is correct.

COPY path for settings.xml must be exactly `settings.xml` — no leading `./`, no subdirectory prefix, no absolute path. Reason: the BuildConfig sets `contextDir: lab1/modernized-ecommerce-app`, which makes that directory the Docker build context root. Both settings.xml and Dockerfile live in that directory, so `COPY settings.xml ...` resolves correctly. Using `./settings.xml`, `/settings.xml`, or any other prefix will cause the build to fail with "file not found".

manifest_label_consistency.description: The `app` label MUST be identical across the Deployment (spec.selector.matchLabels, spec.template.metadata.labels) and the Service (spec.selector). A mismatch means the Service has no endpoints and every request returns 503 even though the pod shows Running.
manifest_label_consistency.rule: Choose one consistent label value for the app (e.g. the participant's repo name such as `lab-app-sgovindan`) and use it in ALL three places: Deployment.spec.selector.matchLabels.app, Deployment.spec.template.metadata.labels.app, and Service.spec.selector.app.
Never use a different label value in the Service selector than what is set on the pod template. Always cross-check before applying the manifest bundle.
manifest_label_consistency.correct_example:
# Deployment spec: selector: matchLabels: app: lab-app-sgovindan # <-- must match Service selector template: metadata: labels: app: lab-app-sgovindan # <-- must match Service selector
# Service spec: selector: app: lab-app-sgovindan # <-- must match Deployment labels
manifest_label_consistency.verification: After ocp.apply_manifest_bundle, confirm the Service has at least one endpoint before calling ocp.start_build. If the Service shows 0 endpoints, the label selector is mismatched — fix the manifests and re-apply before proceeding.

pre_commit_checklist.description: Before running `git add + git commit + git push` to Gitea, verify all of the following. These are things Bob can reason about from the local filesystem — they do not need separate rules.
pre_commit_checklist.check: Every file referenced by a COPY instruction in the Dockerfile actually exists in lab1/modernized-ecommerce-app/ — check with `ls` before writing the Dockerfile.

settings.xml is present in lab1/modernized-ecommerce-app/ and appears in `git status --short` as staged. A missing settings.xml in Gitea causes the OCP build to fail even if the Dockerfile is correct.

The .dockerignore does not accidentally exclude files needed by the build (pom.xml, settings.xml, src/). Review every pattern and confirm it targets only artifacts (target/, surefire-reports/) not source files.

build_config_requirements.description: When applying the BuildConfig manifest via ocp.apply_manifest_bundle, always set ALL of the following fields. Missing any one of them will cause a build failure.
build_config_requirements.note: spec.source.sourceSecret.name is MANDATORY. The secret gitea-credentials is pre-created in every participant namespace by the lab administrator. A BuildConfig without it cannot clone from Gitea and the build fails with "terminal prompts disabled". Never omit it. Never attempt to create the secret yourself.
build_config_requirements.rationale: Setting contextDir scopes the Docker build context to the modernized app directory. This means COPY commands inside the Dockerfile reference paths relative to lab1/modernized-ecommerce-app/, not the repo root — keeping the Dockerfile simple and portable. The sourceSecret is required because the Gitea repository is private and OCP needs credentials to clone it.
build_config_requirements.correct_example: apiVersion: build.openshift.io/v1 kind: BuildConfig metadata: name: lab-app-hbavaria namespace: lab-hbavaria spec: source: type: Git git: uri: https://gitea.apps.itz-qqksk0.infra01-lb.dal14.techzone.ibm.com/lab/lab-app-hbavaria.git ref: main contextDir: lab1/modernized-ecommerce-app sourceSecret: name: gitea-credentials # <-- MANDATORY: pre-existing secret, never create it strategy: type: Docker dockerStrategy: dockerfilePath: Dockerfile output: to: kind: ImageStreamTag name: lab-app-hbavaria:latest

build_trigger_details.description: Triggers a Git-source build by calling the OpenShift BuildConfig instantiate API. OCP clones source directly from the configured Gitea repository — no local file upload occurs and no oc CLI is required.

parameters.param: Name of the BuildConfig resource (e.g. lab-app-hbavaria)

notes.note: Ensure git push to Gitea has completed before calling ocp.start_build, otherwise OCP will clone stale source.
The tool returns the created Build object. Extract the build name (metadata.name) to pass to ocp.wait_for_build.
After successful build and image push, continue with ocp.restart_deployment and ocp.wait_for_deployment.
Never fall back to oc CLI. If ocp.start_build fails due to RBAC or authorization, stop and report the failure.

ocp.wait_for_build has a 60-second timeout. Maven builds typically take 5-10 minutes. If wait_for_build times out with MCP error -32001, do NOT treat this as a build failure. Switch to a polling approach: call ocp.get_pod_logs on the build pod every 30-60 seconds until the log shows BUILD SUCCESS or BUILD FAILURE. Never re-trigger the build on a timeout — the build is still running in OCP.
